Corrected for location of waterspout. The waterspout actually occurred over Griffin Lake (lat: 28.836, lon: -81.841). Videos sent by public and local media show a waterspout developed over Griffin Lake in the Leesburg area near sunset. A confirmed report by the Leesburg fire department, indicates the spout remained over water as it was reaching the ground.

*** 2 FATAL *** Updates previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 E Merritt Island. Emergency management reports crane collapsed at the new Cape Canaveral hospital construction site at Borman and SR-520, leading to 2 fatalities. Storm outflow, producing wind gusts between 45 and 55 mph, were observed near this site at this time.

A video shared by broadcast media taken approximately 3.5 miles down Nova Road from US-192 in the eastbound lane shows a tornado. Radar estimates that the tornado was approximately 2 miles to the east-northeast (28.28N, 81.14W). Based on radar estimates, the tornado formed sometime between 7:15PM and 7:18PM and lifted at 7:23PM.

Preliminary Report. Minor structural and vegetative damage was noted in the vicinity of Airoso Rd. SE and Emerson Dr. in Palm Bay. Witnesses shared photos of a small uprooted oak tree, as well as some damage to a home (fence, shed, as well as some shingles removed from a roof). At this time, based on these reports, and KMLB radar data, a brief EF-0 tornado with peak wind gusts of 65 mph touched in the area. We will continue to work with Brevard County Emergency Management to gather additional information. At this time, a storm survey is not planned.

Public report of minor vegetative damage in Palm Bay near the intersection of Airoso Rd SE & Cherokee Rd SE. A small oak tree was uprooted and several downed tree branches were noted in the area. Time estimated via KMLB radar.
Public video shared by local media showed a waterspout near Ponce Inlet briefly moving onshore before quickly dissipating along the dune line.

A member of the public reported severe flooding within the Brooks Landing neighborhood off of East Jay Jay Road in Titusville.
